text: For the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system initialized with a smoothed bore in u and a KdV soliton in v, does the soliton survive the bore encounter with amplitude >= 0.5 at T=8, and does u remain bounded (|u_max| < 5)?

method: Split operator: Burgers u-eqn via MUSCL+Godunov (TVD, van Leer limiter) + forward Euler; swept-KdV v-eqn via IMEX-CN spectral with 2/3 dealiasing; coupling terms treated explicitly; dt=0.0002, Nx=256, periodic domain [-15,15]
bank_use_rationale: Positive entries select MUSCL+Godunov for bore (prevents Gibbs) and IMEX-CN+2/3-dealiasing for swept-KdV (stable at amp 1.5, dt=5e-4 per G2); negative entries forbid central FD, IFRK4, explicit-only dispersive treatment, and no-dealiasing; dt=2e-4 is more conservative than G2's 5e-4 to account for coupled-system nonlinear CFL tightening.

rationale: Both methods (MUSCL+Godunov for u; fully spectral RK2 for u) produce blow-up at t~1.7. At t=1.14 u already exceeds 5.7 (violates |u_max|<5 target). Root cause: coupling term -d/dx(3v^2+v_xx) resonantly amplifies u even before the soliton meets the bore. Explicit treatment of u with small nu_u=0.002 is insufficient; u grows supercritically from the coupling.

Switch to fully implicit/IMEX treatment for u as well: use IMEX-CN on u's nonlinear term (treating 3u u_x spectrally explicit with dealiasing but adding a larger artificial viscosity nu_u=0.05 in the CN denominator to stabilize the bore), reduce dt to 0.0002, and verify u stays bounded to T=8.

method: Fully spectral IMEX-CN for both u and v with 2/3 dealiasing. u: CN on viscosity term nu_u*u_xx (implicit, stable) + explicit 3uu_x and coupling -d/dx(3v^2+v_xx); v: CN on v_xxx + explicit 6vv_x+d/dx(uv). nu_u=0.05 (physical bore regularization), dt=0.0002, Nx=256.
bank_use_rationale: IMEX-CN is proven stable at amp 1.5 in G2 with dt=5e-4; using dt=2e-4 is more conservative. Adding implicit CN treatment of viscosity nu_u*u_xx keeps u bounded while the bore spreads slightly. Dealiasing mandatory per kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts.

rationale: E2 completes to T=8 with all 8 snapshots finite. Final v_max=0.5797>=0.5 (soliton survived) and all |u_max|<5 (bore bounded). At t=5.714 v temporarily dips to 0.496 but recovers to 0.580 at T=8. The soliton interacts with bore, loses amplitude to 0.67 by T=1.14 and gradually disperses to ~0.58 at final time — it survives. 11 peaks in v at final time indicates the soliton has partially dispersed into radiation train but peak >= 0.5 criterion is met.
E2 satisfies both phenomenon criteria: v_peak>=0.5 and |u_max|<5. Early stop.
